title: Fermented Chive Chewy German Vegetable Crumble
description: A kid-friendly, vegan-friendly fermented dish featuring chopped chives as the main ingredient to create a chewy texture that’s perfect for children.

ingredients.name: cooking oil
ingredients.amount: 2 tablespoons, for coating the mixture before baking

instructions: Mix chopped chives with active dry yeast and water in a bowl. Let it rest at room temperature for about 45 minutes to allow fermentation.
Once done fermenting, mix in maple syrup until thoroughly combined. The mixture should be thickened and bubbly.
Preheat oven to 180°C (350°F). Grease an oven-safe dish with cooking oil evenly.
Fold the fermented chive mixture into a soft dough-like consistency and knead well for about 2 minutes. Divide the dough into small balls.
Roll each ball into thin, flat crumbles in your hands. Place the crumble on the greased baking dish, making sure they are not too close to each other.
Bake the chive crumble at 180°C (350°F) for about 20 minutes or until golden brown and crispy on top.
Remove from oven and let it cool before serving.
